Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Dumping Lego NXT firmware off of an existing brick (2025) 158 upvotes, 11 comments
  2. Lisp-style C++ template meta programming 25 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. CasNum 210 upvotes, 24 comments
  4. Compiling Prolog to Forth [pdf] 100 upvotes, 9 comments
  5. Emacs internals: Deconstructing Lisp_Object in C (Part 2) 17 upvotes, 0 comments
  6. The surprising whimsy of the Time Zone Database 56 upvotes, 6 comments
  7. In 1985 Maxell built a bunch of life-size robots for its bad floppy ad 64 upvotes, 9 comments
  8. Self-Portrait by Ernst Mach (1886) 97 upvotes, 17 comments
  9. macOS code injection for fun and no profit (2024) 78 upvotes, 13 comments
  10. Overheads (2023) 11 upvotes, 0 comments
  11. Israel Strikes Oil Facilities in Iran 10 upvotes, 0 comments
  12. FLASH radiotherapy's bold approach to cancer treatment 186 upvotes, 57 comments
  13. Ki Editor - an editor that operates on the AST 379 upvotes, 136 comments
  14. Effort to prevent government officials from engaging in prediction markets 264 upvotes, 94 comments
  15. The influence of anxiety: Harold Bloom and literary inheritance 17 upvotes, 2 comments
  16. Autoresearch: Agents researching on single-GPU nanochat training automatically 59 upvotes, 16 comments
  17. Show HN: ANSI-Saver – A macOS Screensaver 91 upvotes, 33 comments
  18. LLM Writing Tropes.md 108 upvotes, 46 comments
  19. Will Claude Code ruin our team? 10 upvotes, 1 comments
  20. Bourdieu's theory of taste: a grumbling abrégé (2023) 41 upvotes, 14 comments
  21. Re-creating the complex cuisine of prehistoric Europeans 66 upvotes, 29 comments
  22. Files are the interface humans and agents interact with 188 upvotes, 111 comments
  23. The yoghurt delivery women combatting loneliness in Japan 212 upvotes, 129 comments
  24. Show HN: A weird thing that detects your pulse from the browser video 30 upvotes, 12 comments
  25. Cloud VM benchmarks 2026: performance/price for 44 VM types over 7 providers 85 upvotes, 46 comments
  26. A decade of Docker containers 246 upvotes, 177 comments
  27. Put the zip code first 264 upvotes, 204 comments
  28. The stagnancy of publishing and the disappearance of the midlist 63 upvotes, 43 comments
  29. How important was the Battle of Hastings? 13 upvotes, 13 comments
  30. SigNoz (YC W21) is hiring for engineering, growth and product roles 0 upvotes, 0 comments
